canonical_source_policy: problem.json is the sole runtime source of problem semantics; public_tests.json supplies only public examples

resolved_findings: The canonical description now explicitly covers negative integers, zero, the minus sign, and leading-zero interpretation.
records.proposed_fix: Applied a self-contained semantic description without changing tests or exact comparison.
records.rendered_prompt_summary: Signed 32-bit integer input, ordinary decimal representation, strict bool output, boundaries, entrypoint, and all public examples.

resolved_findings: The former canonical description omitted the m-by-n shape, grid alphabet, open endpoints, allowed right/down moves, and exact meaning of infeasibility.
records.proposed_fix: Applied the README/checker semantics to problem.json without changing feasibility labels or checker behavior.
records.rendered_prompt_summary: Construction dimensions and alphabet, endpoint requirements, right/down path definition, exact path count, infeasible return, constraints, entrypoint, and public feasibility examples.

resolved_findings: The former canonical description did not define either operation, their direction, their preconditions, their unit costs, repeatability, or the reachability rule.
records.proposed_fix: Specified the directed 0-to-1 single-position operation and directed adjacent 11-to-00 operation in problem.json; oracle, tests, and judge remain unchanged.
records.rendered_prompt_summary: Equal-length binary strings, both directed operations and preconditions, unit cost, repeatability, unreachable result, constraints, entrypoint, and four public examples.

resolved_findings: The canonical description now explicitly states i < j, multiset duplicate retention, nondecreasing order, zero-indexing, and output query-order preservation.
records.proposed_fix: Clarified existing semantics without changing the oracle, valid-query contract, tests, or exact comparison.
records.rendered_prompt_summary: Positive integer bounds, every i-less-than-j pair, sorted GCD multiset with duplicates, valid zero-indexed queries, preserved query order, entrypoint, and public examples.

resolved_findings: The canonical description now explicitly defines a swap, distinct arbitrary indices, repeated participation, fewer-than-k including zero swaps, and the non-empty post-swap subarray.
records.proposed_fix: Clarified existing at-most-k swap semantics without changing the oracle, tests, judge, or limits.
records.rendered_prompt_summary: Array and k bounds, arbitrary distinct-index swaps, repeatability, at-most rather than exactly k, non-empty contiguous objective, entrypoint, and public examples.
